# Signal by Frank Meltke

> Practitioner notes on AI governance, agent authorization, and engineering judgment. Written by Frank Meltke while actively building these systems.

Signal is a practitioner notes site by Frank Meltke, founder and CEO of contraco. The notes are written from direct experience building AI agent authorization systems and enterprise AI governance frameworks.

## Notes

- [The Verifier Gap](https://signal.meltke.com/) — Why AI trustworthiness is structural, not technical. The absence of a compiler-equivalent in unbounded domains.
- [What Agent Authorization Actually Requires](https://signal.meltke.com/) — Scope binding, revocation propagation, audit trail architecture for AI agent hierarchies.
- [Engineering Judgment Is the New Compiler](https://signal.meltke.com/) — The scarce resource in enterprise AI deployment when code is abundant.

## About the Author

Frank Meltke is a German inventor, founder and CEO of contraco, guest lecturer at Kyung Hee University in Seoul, and Insight Panelist for BBC Global Minds. He has been building enterprise systems since 1998.

- [Frank Meltke](https://meltke.com)
- [contraco](https://contraco.net)
- [Agentic Commerce Research](https://agentic.contraco.net)
- [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/frankmeltke)
